hypothesis: If molecular embeddings produced by chemical foundation models (SMI-TED, MolT5, Galactica, GraphMVP) are used as inputs to neural networks for predicting battery-relevant properties across molecular, formulation, surface, and device scales, then these models will achieve higher predictive accuracy, better out-of-distribution (tail) generalization, and stronger, more reliable moiety–outcome correlations than models using Morgan fingerprints, because FM pretraining on large SMILES corpora captures richer chemical representations.
